meaning: adj.好笑的,滑稽的,逗趣的n.小丑,小闹剧
examples: Evelyn is entertaining company, with droll and sardonic observations on nearly everything.
In a smaller country this all might be dismissed as quaintly droll.
One was a droll, sarcastic satirist; the other was a celibate mad scientist.
This medium-length film, directed by Letourneur, brings a bracingly droll inventiveness to its tender intrigue.
The company has managed to reintroduce some sexy into the droll, quotidian appliances that fill our lives.
